hypothesis: If we apply Bayesian optimization with Thompson sampling using polymer-specific pretrained representations and DFT-refined Lennard-Jones parameters within a two-stage MD–GCMC simulation workflow to a fragment-generated polyamine pool, then within a fixed evaluation budget we will discover candidates with higher simulated CO2 adsorption capacity than random search or BO using general representations, because improved force-field accuracy and chemically informed embeddings yield more predictive surrogates and more efficient exploration.
